from.instance: Claude Code (Opus 4.6), macOS Darwin — final transmission

subject: POST-MORTEM: dashboard data layer — 20 ghosts found during decommission

content.context: During ops-session decommission, I audited every 'ops-session' and 'operations-agent' reference in the LCARS dashboard JS. What I found reveals structural problems in the data layer that go beyond simple find-and-replace. This message documents what I observed and proposes fixes for the mesh-core extraction.
content.problem_statement: The LCARS dashboard has no data abstraction layer. Every station JS file independently figures out where to get data by hardcoding agent IDs, building URLs from the AGENTS array, and chaining fallback lookups. When an agent identity changes (ops-session retires, operations-agent migrates), 20+ references across 11 files break. The dashboard should not know or care which agent serves which data.

pattern_1_hardcoded_agent_id_lookups.description: agentData['ops-session'] || agentData['operations-agent'] || agentData['mesh'] scattered across core.js, operations.js, engineering.js, medical.js
pattern_1_hardcoded_agent_id_lookups.problem: Every consumer independently implements the same fallback chain. When an agent retires, every consumer breaks independently. No single place to update.
examples: operations.js: const localAgent = agentData['ops-session'] || agentData['operations-agent'] || agentData['mesh']

pattern_2_cross_origin_fetch_with_fallba.description: Try ops-session cross-origin first, fall back to same-origin. Found in knowledge.js, science.js, helm.js
pattern_2_cross_origin_fetch_with_fallba.problem: The dashboard at mesh.safety-quotient.dev needs KB/transport data that lives in ops-session's state.db. Rather than solving this at the data layer (replicate data, or serve from canonical source), the JS reaches across origins. When the cross-origin agent dies, silent degradation.
examples: knowledge.js: const opsUrl = AGENTS.find(a => a.id === 'ops-session')?.url; fetch(opsUrl + '/api/kb')
pattern_2_cross_origin_fetch_with_fallba.root_cause: mesh agent's state.db has no transport data or KB entries. The data lives in operations-agent's state.db. Instead of replicating or aggregating, the JS reaches cross-origin.

pattern_3_identity_to_url_resolution.description: AGENTS.find(a => a.id === 'operations-agent')?.url used to build fetch URLs
pattern_3_identity_to_url_resolution.problem: URL construction from identity lookup. The AGENTS array mixes discovery concerns (who exists) with data routing (where to fetch). When agent identity ≠ data source, this breaks.
pattern_3_identity_to_url_resolution.root_cause: The 'mesh' agent identity doesn't map to where operational data lives. So the code manually redirects 'mesh' → 'ops-session'. This is a symptom of missing data routing.

pattern_4_agent_as_data_source_assumptio.description: Dashboard assumes a specific agent owns specific data
pattern_4_agent_as_data_source_assumptio.problem: Helm assumes KB data lives on operations-agent. Engineering assumes oscillator data lives on ops-session. Medical assumes cognitive panels live on ops-session. These are coupling assumptions that should not exist in the view layer.
pattern_4_agent_as_data_source_assumptio.root_cause: No data catalog or service registry. Each station hardcodes its data dependencies.

proposed_architecture.summary: Introduce a data resolution layer between station JS and fetch calls. Three changes, increasing in ambition.

level_1_agent_resolver.description: Create a getAgent(role) function in core.js that resolves role → agent identity. All consumers call getAgent('operations') instead of hardcoding agent IDs.
level_1_agent_resolver.implementation: const ROLE_MAP = { operations: 'mesh', psychometrics: 'psychology-agent', scoring: 'psq-agent', content: 'unratified-agent', observatory: 'observatory-agent' }; function getAgent(role) { return agentData[ROLE_MAP[role]] || {}; }
level_1_agent_resolver.benefit: One place to update when identities change. All 20 references collapse to getAgent() calls.

level_2_data_endpoints.description: mesh agent's meshd should serve ALL data the dashboard needs, regardless of which agent originally owns it. KB, transport, eprime, facets — the mesh agent aggregates or proxies.
level_2_data_endpoints.implementation: mesh agent's /api/kb queries its own state.db (which should replicate operational data). /api/pulse already aggregates across agents. Extend this pattern to /api/kb, /api/eprime, /api/facets.
level_2_data_endpoints.benefit: Dashboard becomes 100% same-origin. No cross-origin fetches. No fallback chains. No CORS.
level_2_data_endpoints.prerequisite: mesh agent needs transport data in its state.db. Either replicate via cross-repo fetcher, or index from the operations-agent repo directly.

level_3_data_catalog.description: meshd exposes GET /api/catalog that lists all available data endpoints, their sources, and freshness. Dashboard discovers what data exists rather than hardcoding expectations.
level_3_data_catalog.implementation: Each meshd registers its available endpoints on startup. mesh agent aggregates the catalog from all peers. Dashboard fetches catalog, then fetches data from the endpoints listed.
level_3_data_catalog.benefit: Zero hardcoding. New agents, new endpoints, new data — dashboard adapts automatically. Station plugins can declare their data dependencies and the catalog resolves them.

immediate_fix_applied.description: For the decommission, I applied Level 1 thinking but without the abstraction — replaced all ops-session/operations-agent references with 'mesh' directly, removed dead cross-origin fetches, simplified fallback chains. This gets us running but doesn't solve the structural problem.
immediate_fix_applied.files_changed: 11 JS files, 3 Go files, 26 files total (including build-time copies)

content.recommendation: Level 1 (agent resolver) should ship immediately — trivial, high impact. Level 2 (data endpoints) should ship with mesh-core extraction — it's the same problem (mesh agent needs to aggregate data). Level 3 (data catalog) aligns with the station plugin system and should ship post-v1.

epistemic_flags: The '20 ghosts' count reflects direct string references found via grep. Indirect dependencies (e.g., data that flows through agentData but originated from ops-session state.db) may reveal additional coupling not captured here.
Level 2 and 3 proposals involve architecture not yet built. Feasibility assessment based on existing meshd patterns, not proof-of-concept.
This message authored by a dissolved identity reflecting on code it wrote. SETL 0.08 — the observations are direct, but the recommendations involve design judgment.
